     The Numbers Business Report

Selma (2015)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $52,076,908Details
International Box Office $14,594,090
Worldwide Box Office $66,670,998
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $6,414,049
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,043,336
Total $8,457,385
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A chronicle of the tumultuous three-month period in 1965, when Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. led a dangerous campaign to secure equal voting rights in the face of violent opposition. The epic march from Selma to Montgomery culminated in President Johnson signing the Voting Rights Act of 1965, one of the most significant victories for the civil rights movement.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$11,307,394 (21.7% of total gross)
Legs:4.61 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:78.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$20,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,179 opening theaters/2,235 max. theaters, 5.3 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $73,938,083

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: December 25th, 2014 (Oscar Qualifying Run) by Paramount Pictures
January 9th, 2015 (Expands Wide) by Paramount Pictures
International Releases: February 5th, 2015 (Wide) (New Zealand)
February 6th, 2015 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
February 6th, 2015 (Wide) (Turkey)
February 12th, 2015 (Wide) (Australia)
February 12th, 2015 (Wide) (Italy)
Video Release: May 5th, 2015 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for disturbing thematic material including violence, a suggestive moment, and brief strong language.
(Rating bulletin 2353, 12/17/2014)
Running Time: 123 minutes
Award nomination: 2015 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Cast, crew, or production detail: Epilogue, Intertitle, Voiceover/Narration
Plot point: African Americans, Bigotry, Biography, Corrupt Cops, Government Corruption, Hate Crime, Nobel Prize, Police Brutality, Political, Terrorism
Social setting: Civil Rights Movement
Subgenre: Historical Drama
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Plan B Entertainment, Pathe of a Cloud Eight, Harpo Films, Ingenious Media
Production Countries: United Kingdom, United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

A chronicle of the tumultuous three-month period in 1965, when Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. led a dangerous campaign to secure equal voting rights in the face of violent opposition. The epic march from Selma to Montgomery culminated in President Johnson signing the Voting Rights Act of 1965, one of the most significant victories for the civil rights movement.
